title: Linear, neighbor and tree models deterministic AI draft bank

publication.changeSummary: Misconception MCQ, evidence-boundary fill-gap, and deterministic offline Python lab for Linear, neighbor and tree models; all items remain blocked from formal exams pending independent human review.

items.title: Linear, neighbor and tree models: reject the overclaim
items.prompt: Which statement is source-bounded and corrects this AI misconception? A tree is always interpretable, nearest neighbors needs no training choices, and the model with the best validation score is objectively superior.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted unreviewed draft; independent human technical, source, pedagogy, safety, accessibility, and answer-oracle review is required before formal-exam eligibility.

hints: Choose the statement that names mechanism, evidence conditions, and the boundary of the claim rather than a fluent or benchmark-based overgeneralization.

feedback.correct: Correct. Compare model families under one split, preprocessing, metric, uncertainty, and operational-cost plan, then justify assumptions and remaining failure patterns.
feedback.incorrect: That option preserves the overclaim. Compare model families under one split, preprocessing, metric, uncertainty, and operational-cost plan, then justify assumptions and remaining failure patterns.

criteria.description: Selects the source-bounded correction and rejects the documented AI overclaim.

privateAuthorNotes: Human reviewer must verify the correction against cited sources and ensure the distractor does not normalize unsafe or overstated claims.

items.title: Linear, neighbor and tree models: deterministic offline Python lab
items.prompt: Read n one-dimensional training x,label pairs and a query x. Return the label of the nearest point, breaking distance ties by earlier input order.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted unreviewed draft; independent human technical, source, pedagogy, safety, accessibility, and answer-oracle review is required before formal-exam eligibility.

hints: Implement only the stated offline contract, run the visible boundary, and do not infer a real model, safety, fairness, or deployment claim from this toy result.

feedback.correct: The deterministic offline reference checks match the bounded contract; this does not establish real-world AI quality or safety.
feedback.incorrect: At least one deterministic check differs; inspect parsing, algorithm assumptions, boundary behavior, and exact output before retrying.

criteria.description: Passes all visible and hidden deterministic offline tests while preserving the stated evidence and non-claim boundary.

privateAuthorNotes: Offline stdlib-only task. Human reviewer must verify algorithm, fixtures, overclaim boundary, and educational validity before any exam use.

answer.explanation: This transparent one-nearest-neighbor baseline exposes the distance, scaling, locality, and tie assumptions instead of claiming universal superiority.
